stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 30 website users. The sample mean is 364.8 ms with a sample standard deviation of 92.4 ms. However, 2 of the 30 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 28 observations have a mean of 342.3 ms and standard deviation of 61.6 ms. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 364.8, 95% CI = [330.3, 399.3]. Without outliers: mean = 342.3, 95% CI = [319.0, 365.6]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [319.0, 399.3]. Point estimate: 353.6 ms.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [319.0, 399.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
